***I Am Legend*** is a 2007 American [post-apocalyptic](/wiki/Apocalyptic_and_post-apocalyptic_fiction "Apocalyptic and post-apocalyptic fiction") [action horror film](/wiki/Action_horror_film "Action horror film")[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) directed by [Francis Lawrence](/wiki/Francis_Lawrence "Francis Lawrence") from a screenplay by [Akiva Goldsman](/wiki/Akiva_Goldsman "Akiva Goldsman") and [Mark Protosevich](/wiki/Mark_Protosevich "Mark Protosevich") and starring [Will Smith](/wiki/Will_Smith "Will Smith") as [U.S. Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army") [virologist](/wiki/Virology "Virology") Robert Neville. Loosely based on the 1954 [novel of the same name](/wiki/I_Am_Legend_(novel) "I Am Legend (novel)") by [Richard Matheson](/wiki/Richard_Matheson "Richard Matheson"), the film is set in New York City after a virus, which was originally created to cure cancer, has wiped out most of mankind, leaving Neville as the last human in New York City, other than nocturnal mutants. Immune to the virus, Neville works to develop a cure while defending himself against the hostile mutants. It is the third feature-film adaptation of Matheson's novel following 1964's *[The Last Man on Earth](/wiki/The_Last_Man_on_Earth_(1964_film) "The Last Man on Earth (1964 film)")* and 1971's *[The Omega Man](/wiki/The_Omega_Man "The Omega Man")*.

[Warner Bros. Pictures](/wiki/Warner_Bros._Pictures "Warner Bros. Pictures") began developing *I Am Legend* in 1994, and various actors and directors were attached to the project, though production was delayed due to budgetary concerns related to the script. Production began in 2006 in New York City, filming mainly on location in the city, including a $5 million scene[[6]](#cite_note-Reuters-6)[[7]](#cite_note-Steuer-7) at the [Brooklyn Bridge](/wiki/Brooklyn_Bridge "Brooklyn Bridge").

*I Am Legend* was released on December 14, 2007, in the United States and Canada. It opened to the largest-ever box office (not adjusted for inflation) for a non-Christmas film released in the U.S. in December, and was the [seventh-highest-grossing film of 2007](/wiki/2007_in_film#Highest-grossing_films "2007 in film"), earning $256 million domestically and $329 million internationally for a total of $585 million. The film received generally positive reviews, with Smith's performance being singled out for praise, while criticism focused on its divergences from the novel, particularly the ending. In 2022, a sequel was revealed to be in development, with Smith set to reprise his role as Neville and co-produce the film with [Michael B. Jordan](/wiki/Michael_B._Jordan "Michael B. Jordan"), who will also have a starring role.

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

In 2009, a genetically engineered [measles virus](/wiki/Measles_virus "Measles virus") designed to cure [cancer](/wiki/Cancer "Cancer") mutated into a lethal airborne strain called the Krippin Virus, triggering a global pandemic. The virus killed 90% of the human population, and transformed the other 9% into albino vampiric-zombie, albino mutants known as Darkseekers, who roam at night to hunt for immune humans as they are vulnerable to sunlight.

In 2012, three years after the outbreak, [U.S. Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army") [virologist](/wiki/Virologist "Virologist") [LTC](/wiki/Lieutenant_colonel_(United_States) "Lieutenant colonel (United States)") Robert Neville lives an isolated life in a heavily fortified [Washington Square Park](/wiki/Washington_Square_Park,_New_York "Washington Square Park, New York") home in deserted [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan"). Neville's daily routine include experimenting on infected rats to find a cure for the virus, searching for food and supplies in the city, and waiting each day for survivors who might respond to his continuous recorded radio broadcasts, instructing them to meet him at the [South Street Seaport](/wiki/South_Street_Seaport "South Street Seaport") by noon. Neville's only companion is his [German Shepherd](/wiki/German_Shepherd "German Shepherd"), Sam. To cope with his loneliness, he regularly "talks" to [mannequins](/wiki/Mannequins "Mannequins") he positions at video stores. At night, he barricades himself with Sam inside his home to hide from the Darkseekers. Neville is haunted by memories of his wife Zoe, and daughter Marley, who both died in a helicopter accident during the chaotic evacuation of Manhattan under [quarantine](/wiki/Quarantine "Quarantine"). Neville stayed behind with other military personnel to help contain the spread of the virus, taking Sam along with him.

One day, while Neville hunts a deer, Sam pursues it into a building populated with Darkseekers. Neville cautiously locates Sam, and both narrowly escape. Desperate to find a cure, Neville draws out his immune blood to attract a Darkseeker, sets a trap, and captures a female to be used as a subject for his treatment. An alpha Darkseeker tries to pursue them but is halted by the sunlight before retreating into the darkness. Neville begins his treatment on the female Darkseeker in his basement laboratory but seems unsuccessful.

During his city drive, Neville notices "Fred", a mannequin from the video store mysteriously placed outside [Grand Central Terminal](/wiki/Grand_Central_Terminal "Grand Central Terminal"). As he suspiciously approaches Fred, he gets caught in a trap similar to the one used to capture the female Darkseeker, hitting his head and losing consciousness. By [dusk](/wiki/Dusk "Dusk"), he awakes, and the Alpha unleashes infected dogs to attack him. Neville manages to free himself from the trap to subdue the dogs, although Sam is bitten during the struggle. Back in his lab, Neville injects her with his serum, to no avail. Sam begins to transform due to the infection, and Neville strangles her to avoid being infected himself. Grieving for the death of his companion, and on the verge of losing his sanity, Neville assaults the Darkseekers with his car, but is vastly outnumbered. He is rescued by immune survivors: Anna and Ethan, who arrive from [Maryland](/wiki/Maryland "Maryland") after hearing his broadcast.

They take Neville home, where Anna explains they survived the outbreak on a [Red Cross](/wiki/Red_Cross "Red Cross") ship from [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo"), and are heading to a survivors camp in [Bethel, Vermont](/wiki/Bethel,_Vermont "Bethel, Vermont"). Neville disagrees, saying no such camp exists. While working to cure the female Darkseeker, Neville suggests cooling her with ice to improve treatment. Later on, as revenge for the assault at the seaport, the Darkseekers launch an invasion on the house. Neville, Anna, and Ethan retreat to the basement laboratory, sealing themselves with the female test subject. As the Darkseekers attack the lab, Neville discovers his treatment working as the female Darkseeker is slowly turning back to human. The Alpha Darkseeker then enters and begins breaking through the glass door.

### Canonical ending

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Canonical ending")]

As he does so, the Alpha traces the shape of a butterfly on the door; Neville sees that he is copying the tattoo on the female Darkseeker and that they are there to retrieve her as she is the Alpha's mate. Looking around the lab again, Neville sees the wall covered with the photos of his previous Darkseeker experiment subjects and realizes that, to them, he is the monster who has been hunting, capturing and killing them for years. Overcome with guilt and remorse, Neville surrenders and allows the Alpha to take back his mate; they then leave peacefully.

After retrieving all of his data, Neville shuts down the lab and departs New York with Anna and Ethan. Eventually they reach the survivor camp in Bethel and are greeted by other survivors. Neville then hands over the cure before entering the camp with Anna and Ethan.

### Theatrical ending

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Theatrical ending")]

Realizing that the Darkseekers will soon break in, Neville draws a vial of blood from the woman he cured, and gives it to Anna before shutting Ethan and her inside a coal chute in the back of the lab. Neville then sacrifices himself, killing the attacking Darkseekers, including the alpha male, with a grenade.

Anna and Ethan reach the survivor camp in Bethel, greeted by other survivors before Anna gives them the cure. Anna recounts Neville's efforts and sacrifices to save humanity, implying how he became a 'legend'.

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Production")]

### Development

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Development")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6a/I_am_legend.jpg/250px-I_am_legend.jpg)](/wiki/File:I_am_legend.jpg)

[Washington Square](/wiki/Washington_Square_Park,_New_York "Washington Square Park, New York") during production in 2006. In the background is the house where Will Smith's character lives.

The science-fiction [horror](/wiki/Horror_fiction "Horror fiction") genre re-emerged in the late 1990s.[[8]](#cite_note-hughes-8)[*[page needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources")*] In 1995, [Warner Bros. Pictures](/wiki/Warner_Bros._Pictures "Warner Bros. Pictures") began developing the film project, having owned the rights to [Richard Matheson](/wiki/Richard_Matheson "Richard Matheson")'s 1954 novel *[I Am Legend](/wiki/I_Am_Legend_(novel) "I Am Legend (novel)")* since 1970[[9]](#cite_note-variation-9) and having already made the 1971 adaptation *[The Omega Man](/wiki/The_Omega_Man "The Omega Man")*. [Mark Protosevich](/wiki/Mark_Protosevich "Mark Protosevich") was hired to write the script after the studio was impressed with his [speculative script](/wiki/Spec_script "Spec script") of *[The Cell](/wiki/The_Cell_(film) "The Cell (film)")*. Protosevich's first draft took place in 2000 in San Francisco, and contained many similarities with the finished film, though the Darkseekers (called Hemocytes) were civilized to the point of the creatures in *The Omega Man*, and Anna was a lone [morphine](/wiki/Morphine "Morphine") [addict](/wiki/Substance_dependence "Substance dependence"), as well as the fact that a Hemocyte character named Christopher joined forces with Neville. Warner Bros. immediately put the film on the fast track, attaching [Neal H. Moritz](/wiki/Neal_H._Moritz "Neal H. Moritz") as [producer](/wiki/Film_producer "Film producer").[[8]](#cite_note-hughes-8)[*[page needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources")*]

Actors [Tom Cruise](/wiki/Tom_Cruise "Tom Cruise"), [Michael Douglas](/wiki/Michael_Douglas "Michael Douglas"),[[10]](#cite_note-duo-10) and [Mel Gibson](/wiki/Mel_Gibson "Mel Gibson")[[8]](#cite_note-hughes-8)[*[page needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources")*] had been considered to star in the film,[[10]](#cite_note-duo-10) using a script by Protosevich and with [Ridley Scott](/wiki/Ridley_Scott "Ridley Scott") as director; however, by June 1997, the studio's preference was for actor [Arnold Schwarzenegger](/wiki/Arnold_Schwarzenegger "Arnold Schwarzenegger"). In July, Scott and Schwarzenegger finalized negotiations,[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) with production slated to begin the coming September,[[10]](#cite_note-duo-10) using [Houston](/wiki/Houston "Houston") as a stand-in for the film's setting of Los Angeles.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Scott had Protosevich replaced by a screenwriter of his own choosing, [John Logan](/wiki/John_Logan_(writer) "John Logan (writer)"), with whom he spent months of intensive work on a number of different drafts. The Scott–Logan version of *I Am Legend* was a mix of sci-fi and psychological thriller, without dialogue in the first hour and with a sombre ending.[[8]](#cite_note-hughes-8)[*[page needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources")*] The creatures in Logan's version were similar to the Darkseekers of the finished film in their animalistic, barbaric nature. The studio, fearing its lack of commercial appeal and merchandising potential, began to worry about the liberties they had given Scott – then on a negative streak of box-office disappointments – and urged the production team to reconsider the lack of action in the screenplay. After an "esoteric" draft by writer [Neal Jimenez](/wiki/Neal_Jimenez "Neal Jimenez"), Warner Bros. reassigned Protosevich to the project, reluctantly working with Scott again.[[8]](#cite_note-hughes-8)[*[page needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources")*]

In December 1997, the project was called into question when the projected budget escalated to $108 million due to media and shareholder scrutiny of the studio in financing a big-budget film.[[14]](#cite_note-14) Scott rewrote the script to reduce the film's budget by $20 million but in March 1998, the studio canceled the project due to budgetary concerns and quite possibly to the box office failures of Scott's last three films, *[1492: Conquest of Paradise](/wiki/1492:_Conquest_of_Paradise "1492: Conquest of Paradise")*, *[White Squall](/wiki/White_Squall_(film) "White Squall (film)")*, and *[G.I. Jane](/wiki/G.I._Jane "G.I. Jane")*.[[15]](#cite_note-reins-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[8]](#cite_note-hughes-8)[*[page needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources")*] Schwarzenegger's recent films (*[Eraser](/wiki/Eraser_(film) "Eraser (film)")* and Warner Bros.' own *[Batman & Robin](/wiki/Batman_%26_Robin_(film) "Batman & Robin (film)")*) also underperformed, and the studio's latest experiences with big budget sci-fi movies *[Sphere](/wiki/Sphere_(1998_film) "Sphere (1998 film)")* and *[The Postman](/wiki/The_Postman_(film) "The Postman (film)")* were negative as well.[[8]](#cite_note-hughes-8)[*[page needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources")*] In August 1998, director [Rob Bowman](/wiki/Rob_Bowman_(filmmaker) "Rob Bowman (filmmaker)") was attached to the project,[[17]](#cite_note-17) with Protosevich hired to write a third new draft, far more action-oriented than his previous versions but the director (who reportedly wished for [Nicolas Cage](/wiki/Nicolas_Cage "Nicolas Cage") to play the lead) moved on to direct *[Reign of Fire](/wiki/Reign_of_Fire_(film) "Reign of Fire (film)")* and the project did not get off the ground.[[8]](#cite_note-hughes-8)[*[page needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources")*][[18]](#cite_note-18)

In March 2002, Schwarzenegger became the producer of *I Am Legend*, commencing negotiations with [Michael Bay](/wiki/Michael_Bay "Michael Bay") to direct and Will Smith to star in the film.[[19]](#cite_note-latimes-19) Bay and Smith were attracted to the project based on a redraft that would reduce its budget.[[20]](#cite_note-20) The project was shelved due to Warner Bros. president [Alan F. Horn](/wiki/Alan_F._Horn "Alan F. Horn")'s dislike of the script.[[21]](#cite_note-delayed-21) In 2004, [Akiva Goldsman](/wiki/Akiva_Goldsman "Akiva Goldsman") was asked by head of production Jeff Robinov to produce the film.[[22]](#cite_note-nytimes-22) In September 2005, director [Francis Lawrence](/wiki/Francis_Lawrence "Francis Lawrence") signed on to helm the project, with production slated to begin in 2006. [Guillermo del Toro](/wiki/Guillermo_del_Toro "Guillermo del Toro") was originally approached to direct by Smith, but turned it down to direct *[Hellboy II: The Golden Army](/wiki/Hellboy_II:_The_Golden_Army "Hellboy II: The Golden Army")*.[[23]](#cite_note-23) Lawrence, whose film *[Constantine](/wiki/Constantine_(film) "Constantine (film)")* was produced by Goldsman, was fascinated by empty urban environments. He said, "Something's always really excited me about that... to have experienced that much loss, to be without people or any kind of social interaction for that long."[[22]](#cite_note-nytimes-22)

Goldsman took on the project, as he admired the second *I Am Legend* film adaptation, *The Omega Man*.[[24]](#cite_note-ew-24) A rewrite was done to distance the project from the other [zombie](/wiki/Zombie "Zombie") films inspired by the novel,[[19]](#cite_note-latimes-19) as well as from the recently released *[28 Days Later](/wiki/28_Days_Later "28 Days Later"),* although Goldsman was inspired by the scenes of a deserted London in the British horror film to create the scenes of a deserted New York City.[[24]](#cite_note-ew-24) A 40-page scene-by-scene outline of the film was developed by May 2006. When delays occurred on Smith's film *[Hancock](/wiki/Hancock_(film) "Hancock (film)")*, which was scheduled for 2007, it was proposed to switch Smith's films. This meant filming would have to begin in 16 weeks; production was given a green light, using Goldsman's script and the outline.[[22]](#cite_note-nytimes-22) Elements from Protosevich's script were introduced, while the crew consulted with experts on infectious diseases and [solitary confinement](/wiki/Solitary_confinement "Solitary confinement").[[24]](#cite_note-ew-24) Rewrites continued throughout filming, because of Smith's improvisational skills and Lawrence's preference to keep various scenes silent.[[22]](#cite_note-nytimes-22) Lawrence had watched [Jane Campion](/wiki/Jane_Campion "Jane Campion")'s film *[The Piano](/wiki/The_Piano "The Piano")* with a low volume so as to not disturb his newborn son and realized that silence could be very effective cinema.[[25]](#cite_note-empire2-25)

### Casting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Casting")]

Will Smith signed on to play Robert Neville in April 2006.[[26]](#cite_note-reborn-26) He said he took on *I Am Legend* because he felt it could be like "*[Gladiator](/wiki/Gladiator_(2000_film) "Gladiator (2000 film)")* [or] *[Forrest Gump](/wiki/Forrest_Gump "Forrest Gump")*—these are movies with wonderful, audience-pleasing elements, but also uncompromised artistic value. [This] always felt like it had those possibilities to me."[[24]](#cite_note-ew-24) The actor found Neville to be his toughest acting challenge since portraying [Muhammad Ali](/wiki/Muhammad_Ali "Muhammad Ali") in *[Ali](/wiki/Ali_(2001_film) "Ali (2001 film)")* (2001). He said that "when you're on your own, it is kind of hard to find conflict." The film's dark tone and exploration of whether Neville has gone insane during his isolation meant Smith had to restrain himself from falling into a humorous routine during takes.[[27]](#cite_note-empire-27) To prepare for his role, Smith visited the [Centers for Disease Control and Prevention](/wiki/Centers_for_Disease_Control_and_Prevention "Centers for Disease Control and Prevention") in [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_(U.S._state) "Georgia (U.S. state)"). He also met with a person who had been in solitary confinement and a former [prisoner of war](/wiki/Prisoner_of_war "Prisoner of war").[[28]](#cite_note-china-28) Smith compared Neville to [Job](/wiki/Job_(Bible) "Job (Bible)"), who lost his children, livelihood, and health. Like the [Book of Job](/wiki/Book_of_Job "Book of Job"), *I Am Legend* studies the questions, "Can he find a reason to continue? Can he find the hope or desire to excel and advance in life? Or does the death of everything around him create imminent death for himself?"[[19]](#cite_note-latimes-19) He also cited an influence in [Tom Hanks](/wiki/Tom_Hanks "Tom Hanks")' performance in *[Cast Away](/wiki/Cast_Away "Cast Away")* (2000).[[24]](#cite_note-ew-24)

Abbey and Kona, both three-year-old German Shepherd dogs, played Neville's dog Sam.[[29]](#cite_note-29) The rest of the supporting cast consists of [Salli Richardson](/wiki/Salli_Richardson "Salli Richardson") as Zoe, Robert's wife,[[30]](#cite_note-blackfilm-30) and [Alice Braga](/wiki/Alice_Braga "Alice Braga") as a survivor named Anna.[[30]](#cite_note-blackfilm-30) [Willow Smith](/wiki/Willow_Smith "Willow Smith"), Will Smith's daughter, makes her film debut as Marley, Neville's daughter.[[31]](#cite_note-31) [Emma Thompson](/wiki/Emma_Thompson "Emma Thompson") has an uncredited role as Dr. Alice Krippin, who appears on television explaining her cure for cancer that mutates into the virus.[[32]](#cite_note-slate-32) Singer [Mike Patton](/wiki/Mike_Patton "Mike Patton") provided the guttural screams of the infected "hemocytes", and [Dash Mihok](/wiki/Dash_Mihok "Dash Mihok") provided the character animation for the infected "alpha male". Several filler characters with uncredited roles were in old news broadcasts and flashbacks, such as the unnamed President's voice ([Pat Fraley](/wiki/Pat_Fraley "Pat Fraley")), and the cast of *[The Today Show](/wiki/Today_(NBC_program) "Today (NBC program)")*.

### Filming

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Filming")]

[![A below view of a large suspension bridge over a river connecting to an area covered with tall buildings. Another bridge and other buildings can be seen in the background.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f2/Brooklyn_Bridge_-_New_York_City.jpg/250px-Brooklyn_Bridge_-_New_York_City.jpg)](/wiki/File:Brooklyn_Bridge_-_New_York_City.jpg)

The [Brooklyn Bridge](/wiki/Brooklyn_Bridge "Brooklyn Bridge"), where a $5-million scene was filmed

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/55/Marcy_Avenue_Armory_jeh.jpg/250px-Marcy_Avenue_Armory_jeh.jpg)](/wiki/File:Marcy_Avenue_Armory_jeh.jpg)

Marcy Avenue Armory

Akiva Goldsman decided to move the story from Los Angeles to New York City to take advantage of locations that would more easily show emptiness.[[9]](#cite_note-variation-9) Goldsman explained, "L.A. looks empty at three o'clock in the afternoon, [but] New York is never empty . . . it was a much more interesting way of showing the windswept emptiness of the world."[[27]](#cite_note-empire-27) Warner Bros. initially rejected this idea because of the logistics,[[22]](#cite_note-nytimes-22) but Francis Lawrence was determined to shoot on location, to give the film a natural feel that would benefit from not shooting on soundstages. Lawrence went to the city with a [camcorder](/wiki/Camcorder "Camcorder"), and filmed areas filled with crowds. Then, a special effects test was conducted to remove all those people. The test had a powerful effect on studio executives.[[25]](#cite_note-empire2-25) Michael Tadross convinced authorities to close busy areas such as the [Grand Central Terminal](/wiki/Grand_Central_Terminal "Grand Central Terminal") viaduct, several blocks of [Fifth Avenue](/wiki/Fifth_Avenue "Fifth Avenue"), and [Washington Square Park](/wiki/Washington_Square_Park "Washington Square Park").[[22]](#cite_note-nytimes-22) The film was shot primarily in the [anamorphic format](/wiki/Anamorphic_format "Anamorphic format"), with flashback scenes shot in [Super 35](/wiki/Super_35 "Super 35").[[33]](#cite_note-33)

Filming began on September 23, 2006.[[34]](#cite_note-taking-34) The Marcy Avenue Armory in Williamsburg was used for the interior of Neville's home,[[27]](#cite_note-empire-27) while [Greenwich Village](/wiki/Greenwich_Village "Greenwich Village") was used for the exterior.[[19]](#cite_note-latimes-19) Other locations include the Tribeca section of lower Manhattan, the aircraft carrier *[Intrepid](/wiki/USS_Intrepid_(CV-11) "USS Intrepid (CV-11)")*, the [Kingsbridge Armory](/wiki/Kingsbridge_Armory "Kingsbridge Armory") in the Bronx, and St. Patrick's Cathedral.[[9]](#cite_note-variation-9) Weeds were imported from Florida and were strewn across locations to make the city look like it had been overgrown with them.[[22]](#cite_note-nytimes-22) The closure of major streets was controversial with New Yorkers. Will Smith said, "I don't think anyone's going to be able to do that in New York again anytime soon. People were not happy. That's the most middle fingers I've ever gotten in my career."[[19]](#cite_note-latimes-19)

A bridge scene was filmed for six consecutive nights in January on the Brooklyn Bridge to serve as a flashback scene in which New York's citizens evacuate the city. Shooting the scene consumed $5 million of the film's reported $150 million budget, which was likely the most expensive shoot in the city to date. The scene, which had to meet requirements from 14 government agencies, involved 250 crew members and 1,000 extras, including 160 National Guard members.[[6]](#cite_note-Reuters-6)[[7]](#cite_note-Steuer-7)[[35]](#cite_note-35) Also present were several [Humvees](/wiki/Humvee "Humvee"), three [Strykers](/wiki/Stryker "Stryker"), a 110-foot (34 m) [cutter](/wiki/United_States_Coast_Guard_Cutter "United States Coast Guard Cutter"), a 41-foot (12 m) [utility boat](/wiki/USCG_Utility_Boat "USCG Utility Boat"), and two 25-foot (7.6 m) response boat small craft, as well as other vehicles including taxis, police cars, fire trucks, and ambulances.[[6]](#cite_note-Reuters-6) Filming concluded on March 31, 2007.[[34]](#cite_note-taking-34) [Computer-generated imagery](/wiki/Computer-generated_imagery "Computer-generated imagery") (CGI) was used to depict the main spans of the Brooklyn Bridge and the [Manhattan Bridge](/wiki/Manhattan_Bridge "Manhattan Bridge") collapsing as missiles from passing military jets blew them up to quarantine Manhattan island.

The end of the film was shot in [Lambertville, New Jersey](/wiki/Lambertville,_New_Jersey "Lambertville, New Jersey").[[36]](#cite_note-36)

Reshoots were conducted around November 2007. Lawrence noted, "We weren't seeing fully rendered shots until about a month ago. The movie starts to take on a whole other life. It's not until later that you can judge a movie as a whole and go, 'Huh, maybe we should shoot this little piece in the middle, or tweak this a little bit.' It just so happened that our reshoots revolved around the end of the movie."[[37]](#cite_note-37)

### Effects

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Effects")]

A week into filming, Lawrence felt the infected (referred to as "Darkseekers" or "hemocytes" in the script), who were being portrayed by actors wearing prosthetics, were not convincing. His decision to use CGI resulted in an increased budget and extended [post-production](/wiki/Post-production "Post-production"), although the end results were not always well received.[[38]](#cite_note-timesreview-38)[[39]](#cite_note-rtreview-39) The concept behind the infected was that their [adrenal glands](/wiki/Adrenal_gland "Adrenal gland") were open all of the time, and Lawrence explained:

> "They needed to have an abandon in their performance that you just can't get out of people in the middle of the night when they're barefoot. And their metabolisms are really spiked, so they're constantly [hyperventilating](/wiki/Hyperventilation "Hyperventilation"), which you can't really get actors to do for a long time or they pass out."

— Francis Lawrence[[22]](#cite_note-nytimes-22)

The actors remained on set to provide [motion capture](/wiki/Motion_capture "Motion capture").[[27]](#cite_note-empire-27)[[40]](#cite_note-40) "The film's producers and sound people wanted the creatures in the movie to sound somewhat human, but not the standard", so Mike Patton, lead singer of [Faith No More](/wiki/Faith_No_More "Faith No More"), was engaged to provide the screams and howls of the infected.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

In addition, CGI was used for the lions and [deer](/wiki/Deer "Deer") in the film, and to erase pedestrians in shots of New York. Workers visible in windows, spectators, and moving cars in the distance were all removed. In his vision of an empty New York, Lawrence cited [John Ford](/wiki/John_Ford "John Ford") as his influence:

> "We didn't want to make an apocalyptic movie where the landscape felt apocalyptic. A lot of the movie takes place on a beautiful day. There's something magical about the empty city as opposed to dark and scary that was the ideal that the cast and crew wanted."

— Francis Lawrence (2007)[[24]](#cite_note-ew-24)

## Music

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Music")]

Main article: [I Am Legend (soundtrack)](/wiki/I_Am_Legend_(soundtrack) "I Am Legend (soundtrack)")

The soundtrack for *I Am Legend* was released on January 15, 2008, under the record label [Varèse Sarabande](/wiki/Var%C3%A8se_Sarabande "Varèse Sarabande"). The music was composed by [James Newton Howard](/wiki/James_Newton_Howard "James Newton Howard") and performed by the [Hollywood Studio Symphony](/wiki/Hollywood_Studio_Symphony "Hollywood Studio Symphony"). The film also features [Bob Marley](/wiki/Bob_Marley "Bob Marley") songs "[Redemption Song](/wiki/Redemption_Song "Redemption Song")", "[Three Little Birds](/wiki/Three_Little_Birds "Three Little Birds")", "[Buffalo Soldier](/wiki/Buffalo_Soldier_(song) "Buffalo Soldier (song)")" and "[Stir It Up](/wiki/Stir_It_Up "Stir It Up")". Marley is also discussed in the film, and Neville's daughter is named after him.[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-43)

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Release")]

### Theatrical

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Theatrical")]

*I Am Legend* was originally slated for a November 21, 2007, release in the United States and Canada,[[44]](#cite_note-44) but was delayed to December 14.[[45]](#cite_note-45) The film opened on December 26, 2007, in the United Kingdom,[[46]](#cite_note-46) and Ireland, having been originally scheduled for January 4, 2008.[[27]](#cite_note-empire-27)

In December 2007, China temporarily suspended the release of all American films in the country,[[47]](#cite_note-47) which is believed to have delayed the release of *I Am Legend*. Will Smith spoke to the chairman of [China Film Group](/wiki/China_Film_Group "China Film Group") about securing a release date, later explaining, "We struggled very, very hard to try to get it to work out, but there are only a certain amount of foreign films that are allowed in."[[28]](#cite_note-china-28)

Premieres were held in Tokyo, New York, and London. At the London premiere in [Leicester Square](/wiki/Leicester_Square "Leicester Square"), British comedian and actor [Neg Dupree](/wiki/Neg_Dupree "Neg Dupree") was arrested after pushing his way onto the red carpet and running around shouting "I am Negend!".[[48]](#cite_note-48) The stunt was part of his "Neg's Urban Sports" section of comedy game show *[Balls of Steel](/wiki/Balls_of_Steel_(TV_series) "Balls of Steel (TV series)")*.

### Marketing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Marketing")]

The film's [teaser trailer](/wiki/Teaser_trailer "Teaser trailer") was attached to the screenings of *[Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix](/wiki/Harry_Potter_and_the_Order_of_the_Phoenix_(film) "Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix (film)")*. And a tie-in comic from [DC Comics](/wiki/DC_Comics "DC Comics") and [Vertigo Comics](/wiki/Vertigo_Comics "Vertigo Comics") was created, *I Am Legend: Awakening*.[[49]](#cite_note-49) The project drew upon collaboration from [Bill Sienkiewicz](/wiki/Bill_Sienkiewicz "Bill Sienkiewicz"), screenwriter Mark Protosevich, and author [Orson Scott Card](/wiki/Orson_Scott_Card "Orson Scott Card"). The son of the original book's author, Richard Christian Matheson, also collaborated on the project. The project advanced from the comic to an online format in which animated featurettes (created by the team from [Broken Saints](/wiki/Broken_Saints "Broken Saints")) were shown on the official website.[[50]](#cite_note-50)

In October 2007, Warner Bros. Pictures, in conjunction with the Electric Sheep Company, launched the online multiplayer game *I Am Legend: Survival* in the virtual world *[Second Life](/wiki/Second_Life "Second Life")*. The game was the largest launched in the virtual world in support of a film release, permitting people to play against each other as the infected or the uninfected across a replicated 60 acres (240,000 m2) of New York City.[[51]](#cite_note-51) The studio also hired the ad agency Crew Creative to develop a website that was specifically viewable on the [iPhone](/wiki/IPhone "IPhone").[[52]](#cite_note-52)

### Home media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Home media")]

The film was released on [DVD](/wiki/DVD "DVD") on March 18, 2008, by [Warner Home Video](/wiki/Warner_Home_Video "Warner Home Video"), in two editions: a one-disc release, including the movie with four animated comics ("Death As a Gift", "Isolation", "Sacrificing the Few for the Many", and "Shelter"), and other [DVD-ROM](/wiki/DVD-ROM "DVD-ROM") features, and a two-disc special edition that includes all these extras, an alternative theatrical version of the movie with an ending that follows closer to that from the novel,[[53]](#cite_note-ign-53) and a [digital copy](/wiki/Digital_copy "Digital copy") of the film.[[54]](#cite_note-54) On the high-definition end, the movie has been released on the [Blu-ray Disc](/wiki/Blu-ray_Disc "Blu-ray Disc") format and [HD DVD](/wiki/HD_DVD "HD DVD") format along with the DVD release, with the HD-DVD version being released later on April 8, 2008.[[55]](#cite_note-Charles_Lyons-55) Both HD releases include all the features available in the two-disc DVD edition.[[55]](#cite_note-Charles_Lyons-55) A three-disk Ultimate Collector's Edition was also released on December 9, 2008.[[56]](#cite_note-56) On December 6, 2016, the movie was released on [Ultra HD Blu-ray](/wiki/Ultra_HD_Blu-ray "Ultra HD Blu-ray") as a combo-pack. This version featured the theatrical version in [4K](/wiki/4K_resolution "4K resolution"), and a standard Blu-ray disc with both versions of the movie and all the previous extras. It also included a digital copy of the theatrical version in HD.[[57]](#cite_note-57)

The film has sold 7.04 million DVDs and earned $126.2 million in revenue, making it the sixth-best-selling DVD of 2008.[[58]](#cite_note-58) However, Warner Bros. was reportedly "a little disappointed" with the film's performance on the DVD market.[[59]](#cite_note-59)

An [alternative ending](/wiki/Alternative_ending "Alternative ending"), which had been removed from the theatrical cut due to negative response from initial [test screening](/wiki/Test_screening "Test screening") audiences, was released on the two-disc special edition DVD of *I Am Legend* in 2008:[[53]](#cite_note-ign-53)[[60]](#cite_note-60)[[61]](#cite_note-61)[[62]](#cite_note-62)[[63]](#cite_note-63)[[64]](#cite_note-64)

> As the Darkseekers attack the lab; the alpha male creates a butterfly shape while trying to break through the glass. Neville recognizes it as a reference to the butterfly tattoo on the female Darkseeker's neck, realizing the alpha male and his followers just want to recover his mate. Neville puts down his gun, returns the female, and they stare each other down. Neville apologizes after seeing the alpha's emotional response. The alpha departs with his pack. Neville, shocked, looks at photos of his test subjects and feels he has become a monster, remorseful for his experiments. The next morning, Neville leaves his research with Anna and Ethan for Vermont to find the survivors' colony. They cross the [George Washington Bridge](/wiki/George_Washington_Bridge "George Washington Bridge") as Anna concludes with: 'You are not alone.'

In February 2023, it was announced that the sequel to *I Am Legend* would disregard the theatrical ending and instead serve as a follow-up to the alternative ending, with Smith reprising his role as Neville as he resumes his experiments on the Darkseekers.[[65]](#cite_note-Sequel-65)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Reception")]

### Box office

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Box office")]

*I Am Legend* collected $77.2 million during its opening weekend, ranking in first place at the box office above *[Alvin and the Chipmunks](/wiki/Alvin_and_the_Chipmunks_(film) "Alvin and the Chipmunks (film)")*.[[66]](#cite_note-66) It surpassed *[The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King](/wiki/The_Lord_of_the_Rings:_The_Return_of_the_King "The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King")* to have the highest December opening weekend.[[67]](#cite_note-LivesOn-67) That record would be surpassed by *[The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey](/wiki/The_Hobbit:_An_Unexpected_Journey "The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey")* five years later in 2012.[[68]](#cite_note-68)

The film grossed $256 million domestically and $329 million internationally, increasing the total gross to $585.4 million.[[3]](#cite_note-boxofficemojo.com-3)

### Critical response

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Critical response")]

On [Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes") the film has an approval rating of 68% based on 213 reviews with an average rating of 6.34/10. The site's critical consensus reads, "*I Am Legend* overcomes questionable special effects and succeeds largely on the strength of Will Smith's mesmerizing performance."[[69]](#cite_note-69) On [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic"), the film has a [weighted average](/wiki/Weighted_arithmetic_mean "Weighted arithmetic mean") score of 65 out of 100, based on 37 critics, indicating "generally favorable" reviews.[[70]](#cite_note-meta-70) Audiences polled by [CinemaScore](/wiki/CinemaScore "CinemaScore") gave the film an average grade of "B" on an A+ to F scale.[[71]](#cite_note-71)

[A. O. Scott](/wiki/A._O._Scott "A. O. Scott") wrote that Will Smith gave a "graceful and effortless performance" and also noted the "third-act collapse". He felt that the movie "does ponder some pretty deep questions about the collapse and persistence of human civilization".[[72]](#cite_note-72) [Dana Stevens](/wiki/Dana_Stevens_(critic) "Dana Stevens (critic)") of *[Slate](/wiki/Slate_(magazine) "Slate (magazine)")* wrote that the movie lost its way around the hour mark, noting that "the Infected just aren't that scary."[[32]](#cite_note-slate-32) [NPR](/wiki/NPR "NPR") critic [Bob Mondello](/wiki/Bob_Mondello "Bob Mondello") noted the film's subtext concerning global [terrorism](/wiki/Terrorism "Terrorism") and that this aspect made the film fit in perfectly with other, more direct cinematic explorations of the subject.[[73]](#cite_note-73) [Richard Roeper](/wiki/Richard_Roeper "Richard Roeper") gave the film a positive review on the television program *[At the Movies with Ebert & Roeper](/wiki/At_the_Movies_with_Ebert_%26_Roeper "At the Movies with Ebert & Roeper")*, commending Will Smith as being in "prime form", also saying there are "some amazing sequences" and that there was "a pretty heavy screenplay for an action film."[[74]](#cite_note-74) In his review for the *[Chicago Sun-Times](/wiki/Chicago_Sun-Times "Chicago Sun-Times")*, [Roger Ebert](/wiki/Roger_Ebert "Roger Ebert") gave the film three stars out of four, writing, "Given its setup, *I Am Legend* is well-constructed to involve us with Dr. Neville and his campaign to survive."[[75]](#cite_note-75) The film has been criticized for diverging from Matheson's novel, especially in its portrayal of a specifically Christian theme.[[76]](#cite_note-76) Much of the negative criticism concerned the film's third [act](/wiki/Act_(theatre)#Act_three "Act (theatre)"),[[38]](#cite_note-timesreview-38)[[39]](#cite_note-rtreview-39)[[77]](#cite_note-timereview-77) with some critics favoring the alternative ending in the DVD release.[[53]](#cite_note-ign-53)

### Other response

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Other response")]

*[Popular Mechanics](/wiki/Popular_Mechanics "Popular Mechanics")* published an article on December 14, 2007,[[78]](#cite_note-78) addressing some of the scientific issues raised by the film:

1. the rate of deterioration of urban structures, infrastructure, and survival of [fauna](/wiki/Fauna "Fauna") and [flora](/wiki/Flora "Flora")
2. the plausibility of a [retrovirus](/wiki/Retrovirus "Retrovirus") spreading out of control as depicted in the film (the [measles](/wiki/Measles "Measles") virus depicted in the film is not a retrovirus, but is in fact a part of the *[Paramyxovirus](/wiki/Paramyxoviridae "Paramyxoviridae")* family)
3. the mechanics of the Brooklyn Bridge's destruction

The magazine solicited reactions from [Alan Weisman](/wiki/Alan_Weisman "Alan Weisman"), author of *[The World Without Us](/wiki/The_World_Without_Us "The World Without Us")*, [virologist](/wiki/Virology "Virology") [W. Ian Lipkin](/wiki/W._Ian_Lipkin "W. Ian Lipkin"), [MD](/wiki/Doctor_of_Medicine "Doctor of Medicine"), and Michel Bruneau, PhD, comparing their predictions with the film's depictions. The article raised the most questions regarding the virus' mutation and the medical results, and pointed out that a [suspension bridge](/wiki/Suspension_bridge "Suspension bridge") like the Brooklyn Bridge would likely completely collapse rather than losing only its middle span. Neville's method of producing power using gasoline-powered generators seemed the most credible: "This part of the tale is possible, if not entirely likely," *Popular Mechanics* editor Roy Berendsohn says.

Marxist philosopher [Slavoj Žižek](/wiki/Slavoj_%C5%BDi%C5%BEek "Slavoj Žižek") criticized the film politically for, in his view, "being the most [regressive](/wiki/Reactionary "Reactionary") adaptation from the novel." He said that while the original novel had a "progressive [multicultural](/wiki/Multiculturalism "Multiculturalism") message" where Neville became a "legend" to the new creatures and is subsequently killed by them (much like vampire were legends to humans), the 2007 film finds a cure for the Darkseekers and it is delivered by a survivor through ["divine intervention".](/wiki/Miracle "Miracle") For Žižek, this misses the original message and "openly opt[s] for [religious fundamentalism](/wiki/Religious_fundamentalism "Religious fundamentalism")."[[79]](#cite_note-79)

### Accolades

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Accolades")]

*I Am Legend* earned four nominations for the [Visual Effects Society](/wiki/Visual_Effects_Society "Visual Effects Society") awards,[[80]](#cite_note-80) and was also nominated for [Outstanding Performance by a Stunt Ensemble](/wiki/Screen_Actors_Guild_Award_for_Outstanding_Performance_by_a_Stunt_Ensemble_in_a_Motion_Picture "Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Performance by a Stunt Ensemble in a Motion Picture") at the [Screen Actors Guild Awards](/wiki/Screen_Actors_Guild_Awards "Screen Actors Guild Awards"),[[81]](#cite_note-81) Outstanding Film and Actor at the [Image Awards](/wiki/Image_Awards "Image Awards"),[[82]](#cite_note-82) and Best Sound at the [Satellite Awards](/wiki/Satellite_Awards "Satellite Awards"). In June 2008, Will Smith won a [Saturn Award for Best Actor](/wiki/Saturn_Award_for_Best_Actor "Saturn Award for Best Actor").[[83]](#cite_note-83)
Will Smith also won the [MTV Movie Awards](/wiki/MTV_Movie_Awards "MTV Movie Awards") for Best Male Performance.[[84]](#cite_note-84)

## Future

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Am_Legend_(film)&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Future")]

Director [Francis Lawrence](/wiki/Francis_Lawrence "Francis Lawrence") said in 2008 that there would be a prequel and that Will Smith would be reprising his role. The plot of the film would reveal what happened to Neville before the infected took over New York. [D. B. Weiss](/wiki/D._B._Weiss "D. B. Weiss") was hired to write the script, while Lawrence was in negotiations to return as director, contingent on a sufficiently interesting story. Smith later discussed the premise, which would have his character and a team going from New York City to Washington, D.C., as they made their last stand against those infected with the virus.[[85]](#cite_note-85) The film would again explore the premise of being alone. Lawrence stated, "... the tough thing is, how do we do that again and in a different way?"[[86]](#cite_note-86) In May 2011, Lawrence stated that the prequel was no longer in development saying, "I don't think that's ever going to happen."[[87]](#cite_note-87)

In 2012, Warner Bros. announced that negotiations had begun to produce another installment with the intention of having Smith reprise his role.[[88]](#cite_note-88) In April 2014, the studio acquired a script entitled *A Garden at the End of the World*, described as a post-apocalyptic variation of *[The Searchers](/wiki/The_Searchers "The Searchers")*. Studio executives found so many similarities to *I Am Legend* in the screenplay, they had the author Gary Graham rewrite it so it could serve as a reboot of the story, hoping to create a franchise with the new film. By 2014, Smith, who is known for his reluctance to appear in sequels, had not commented on whether he would appear.[[89]](#cite_note-ial-89)

On March 4, 2022, a sequel was officially announced as in development, with Will Smith reprising his role and [Michael B. Jordan](/wiki/Michael_B._Jordan "Michael B. Jordan") set to star, and both Will Smith and Michael B. Jordan will be producing. Akiva Goldsman would also return to write the script.[[90]](#cite_note-90) The film will follow up on the alternative ending, rather than the theatrical.[[65]](#cite_note-Sequel-65)

On July 25, 2024, Goldsman announced that a second draft had been written. One day later, [Steven Caple Jr.](/wiki/Steven_Caple_Jr. "Steven Caple Jr.") was reported to be in talks to direct the sequel.[[91]](#cite_note-91)
